diagrams.title: Virus vs Worm vs Trojan vs Bot Comparison
diagrams.description: A comparison showing propagation methods and characteristics of four major malware types
elements: Virus: Requires host file, human action, attaches to programs
Worm: Self-replicating, no host needed, spreads via network
Trojan: Disguised as legitimate, creates backdoors, no replication
Bot: Compromised system in botnet, automated commands
diagrams.purpose: Understand key differences in how each malware type spreads and operates

diagrams.title: Botnet Architecture
diagrams.description: Diagram showing how bots connect to C2 servers and are used in coordinated attacks
diagrams.purpose: Visualize how botnets are structured and controlled for attacks

diagrams.title: Malware Infection Lifecycle
diagrams.description: Stages showing how malware enters, establishes, spreads, and maintains control over systems
diagrams.purpose: Understand the complete process of a malware attack
